accepted_claims: synthetic ordinary-CFS test-only denial path completed the 0186 QEMU negative workload
the denied synthetic comm-prefix task was not observed as sched_switch next_comm in that workload
the allowed sibling was observed and completed in that workload

design_findings.summary: post-filter fallback can require progress over ordinary CFS eligibility
design_findings.required_resolution: production design needs picker-visible eligibility or an explicitly modeled bounded fallback with fairness and latency proof

design_findings.summary: fallback scan walks the CFS rb-tree when denial blockage is active
design_findings.required_resolution: production design needs no unbounded O(n) hot-path scan or a separately accepted bounded data structure

design_findings.summary: coverage is ordinary-CFS-only and excludes core scheduling, proxy execution, sched_ext, and DL fair-server claims
design_findings.required_resolution: each excluded path needs explicit support, disablement, or exclusion before broad runtime coverage claims

design_findings.summary: denial receipt capacity and retry count are intentionally bounded to one, so the draft cannot claim complete CFS deny-and-repick
design_findings.required_resolution: production design must either prove bounded capacity sufficient for a scoped claim or define a complete picker-visible selection structure

design_findings.summary: synthetic denial predicate uses task->comm and can race with task-name changes; it is not authority
design_findings.required_resolution: replace test predicate with real frozen authority before any production denial claim

next_actions: record validation 0187 security and overclaim boundary review
treat patch queue metadata/style cleanup as a separate maintenance operation
design production-quality picker-visible lease eligibility before claiming P5A-R acceptance
